business_summary: This database supports a multi-department operations platform for an industrial/manufacturing company (steel/rolling mill context). It combines (1) internal task management (checklists, delegations, ad-hoc assignments, maintenance/repair tasks), (2) plant production/quality operational logs (SMS register, ladle/tundish/patching checklists, hot coil, pipe mill, recoiler), (3) store & purchase workflows (indents, GRN approvals, repair follow-ups), (4) HR/administration workflows (users, leave requests, visitor/gate pass management, travel/ticket booking, hiring requests), (5) sales/CRM processes (leads, enquiries, quotations, enquiry-to-order tracking, client follow-ups), and (6) subscription/document/approval tracking (subscriptions, renewals, payments, document repository). The schema is operationally oriented with many workflow stage dates (planned vs actual), delay metrics, and approval/closure flags; several modules store denormalized text fields (names instead of user IDs) and JSON payloads for line items.

enquiry_to_order.description: Operational CRM record representing a sales enquiry and its progression toward an order, including follow-ups, customer feedback, payment/transport terms, and hold/rejection reasons.
foreign_keys: Potential link to enquiries/leads via en_enquiry_no (matches enquiry_no / lead_no patterns in other tables, not enforced)
Potential link to users via sales_person_name / enquiry_receiver_name / sales_coordinator_name (stored as names; no FK constraint)

column_descriptions.id: Surrogate identifier for the enquiry-to-order record.
column_descriptions.lead_source: Channel/source of the lead/enquiry (Telephonic, Google, Direct Visit, etc.).
column_descriptions.company_name: Customer/company name associated with the enquiry.
column_descriptions.phone_number: Primary phone contact for the enquiry.
column_descriptions.sales_person_name: Salesperson responsible for the enquiry (stored as name).
column_descriptions.enquiry_receiver_name: Person who initially received the enquiry (stored as name).
column_descriptions.enquiry_approach: How enquiry was received/handled (INWARD, Phone Call, etc.).
column_descriptions.item_qty: JSON-like text list of requested items and quantities.
column_descriptions.planned: Planned completion/next milestone date for enquiry stage.
column_descriptions.actual: Actual completion date for planned milestone.
column_descriptions.delay: Delay in days between planned and actual (nullable; sometimes computed elsewhere).
column_descriptions.is_order_received: Whether order has been received from customer.
column_descriptions.status: Generic status field (may overlap with enquiry_status).
column_descriptions.if_no_relevant_reason_status: Reason category when order not received (or status not progressed).
column_descriptions.if_no_relevant_reason_remark: Free-text reason details when order not received.
column_descriptions.customer_order_hold_reason_category: Reason category when customer puts order on hold.
column_descriptions.holding_date: Date when enquiry/order was put on hold.
column_descriptions.planned_days: Planned number of days for the stage/cycle.

delegation.description: Delegated task assignments (typically one-time) with planned/submission dates, reminder/attachment requirements, and completion/extension status.
foreign_keys: Potential link to users via name (assignee) and given_by (assigner) stored as text
Potential link to delegation_done via task_id (completion log)

column_descriptions.task_id: Unique identifier for the delegated task.
column_descriptions.created_at: When the delegation record was created.
column_descriptions.name: Assignee name (doer) for the task.
column_descriptions.task_start_date: Scheduled start datetime of the task.
column_descriptions.image: Image/attachment URL related to the task.
column_descriptions.planned_date: Planned completion datetime (often same as start for one-time).
column_descriptions.color_code_for: UI color/status coding key (likely for dashboards).
column_descriptions.delay: Interval difference between planned and submission/completion.

delegation_done.description: Event/log table capturing completion/extension actions for delegated tasks, including reason and evidence.
foreign_keys: task_id -> delegation.task_id (logical, not enforced)

column_descriptions.created_at: When this status update was logged.
column_descriptions.next_extend_date: New due date if task was extended.
column_descriptions.reason: Reason for extension or completion notes.
column_descriptions.name: Assignee name.
column_descriptions.task_description: Snapshot of task description at time of update.
column_descriptions.id: UUID primary key for the log record.

users.description: Application user/employee master for authentication and access control across multiple systems/modules.
foreign_keys: Potential link to leave_request.user_id -> users.id (appears intended)
Other tables store names/employee_id rather than users.id; join often by employee_id or user_name

column_descriptions.password: Password (appears stored in plain text; security risk).
column_descriptions.given_by: Creator/approver name who created the account.

checklist.description: Recurring or scheduled checklist tasks (daily/weekly/monthly etc.) completed by users with optional admin confirmation and attachments.
foreign_keys: Potential link to users via name (assignee) and given_by stored as text

column_descriptions.department: Department responsible for the checklist task.
column_descriptions.given_by: Person who assigned/defined the checklist item.
column_descriptions.name: Assignee/doer name.
column_descriptions.remark: Remarks or notes for the task instance.
column_descriptions.delay: Interval between planned/scheduled and submission time.
column_descriptions.planned_date: Planned date/time (stored as TEXT; likely schedule).
column_descriptions.created_at: Creation timestamp for the checklist record.
column_descriptions.task_start_date: Scheduled start time for a particular occurrence.
column_descriptions.user_status_checklist: User submitted status (Yes/No) separate from admin_done.

assign_task.description: General task assignment table (often housekeeping/area tasks) with scheduling, submission, delay, and proof attachments.
foreign_keys: task_id is an external/business identifier; in sample equals id
Potential link to users via given_by/name/doer_name2 text fields

column_descriptions.id: Primary key for the task assignment record.
column_descriptions.task_id: Task identifier (string) used by UI/business; often same as id.
column_descriptions.department: Physical area/location where task applies (not HR department).
column_descriptions.name: Role/group name (e.g., Housekeeping Staff).
column_descriptions.delay: Delay in minutes/hours (stored as integer; semantics unclear).

maintenance_task_assign.description: Preventive/regular maintenance task assignments for machines/assets, including condition checks (sound/temperature), scheduling, completion, and cost capture.
foreign_keys: serial_no may relate to form_responses.serial_no/tag_no (asset registry), not enforced
Potential link to users via given_by/doer_name stored as text

column_descriptions.serial_no: Asset/equipment serial number or tag identifier.
column_descriptions.machine_area: Plant area/unit where machine is located.
column_descriptions.delay: Delay/duration text between scheduled and actual.

repair_system.description: Repair workflow for machines/components including vendor dispatch, transport, billing, staged timelines (planned/actual/delay), and payment reconciliation.
foreign_keys: serial_no may link to form_responses.serial_no/tag_no (asset registry), not enforced
Potential link to ticket_book / payment_fms (conceptual) via bill fields; not enforced

column_descriptions.planned_1: Stage 1 planned datetime (e.g., dispatch to vendor).

indent.description: Store & purchase indent/requisition line items capturing requested material, quantities, approvals, and timing (planned vs actual) including GM approval.
foreign_keys: Potential link to indent_approvals.indent_id -> indent.id
item_code may link to dropdown.item_code or ERP item master (not present)
requester_name may link to users (by name/employee_id; not enforced)

column_descriptions.id: Primary key for indent line record.
column_descriptions.time_delay_1: Delay duration between planned and actual.

indent_approvals.description: Approval actions for indents, capturing approver, status, timestamp, and remarks.
foreign_keys: indent_id -> indent.id (intended)
approver_id may link to users.employee_id or users.id (stored as varchar; unclear)

column_descriptions.approver_id: Identifier of approver (employee code or user id).

store_grn.description: Goods Receipt Note (GRN) header-level tracking with bill workflow flags (sent, admin approved, GM approved, closed).
foreign_keys: Potential link to vendor/party master (not present)
Potential link to indent/purchase order modules (not present here)

column_descriptions.planned_date: Planned timestamp for GRN/bill processing milestone.
column_descriptions.sended_bill: Whether bill was sent to accounts/admin.

visitors.description: Visitor gate-pass log for external visitors, including approvals, entry/exit times, and closure state.
foreign_keys: person_to_meet may link to person_to_meet.person_to_meet (text match; not enforced)
approved_by could link to users.user_name (text match; not enforced)

person_to_meet.description: Master list of employees/persons that visitors can select to meet.

gatepass.description: Employee gate pass / short leave/outpass record (internal), including HOD approval and closure.
foreign_keys: Potential link to users by name/department/employee_id (employee_id not present here; not enforced)

column_descriptions.name: Employee name.
column_descriptions.time_of_entry: Time of entry/exit (field name suggests entry; used for gate pass time).

leave_request.description: Employee leave application workflow with multi-level approvals (manager/HOD, HR, commercial head).
foreign_keys: user_id -> users.id (intended; nullable)
employee_id may match users.employee_id (text match; not enforced)

column_descriptions.user_id: Foreign key to users table (sometimes null).

systems.description: Master catalog of systems/modules with URLs for navigation (used for access control and system launching).

documents.description: Central document repository/metadata store for company and personal documents with renewal tracking.

column_descriptions.document_id: Primary key for the document record.

sharedocuments.description: Outbound document sharing log: who shared what document, to which email/number, and by which method/source sheet.
foreign_keys: serial_no may reference documents.serial_no (string vs bigint mismatch; not enforced)

subscription.description: Subscription contract tracker for services/software with staged planned/actual dates, approvals, renewal counts, and document copies.
foreign_keys: subscription_no referenced by approval_history, payment_history, subscription_renewals (logical)
company_name may link to subscription_master.company_name (not enforced)

column_descriptions.planned_1: Planned date for stage 1 (e.g., request/initial approval).

subscription_renewals.description: Renewal events for subscriptions including approver, renewal status, and price at renewal time.
foreign_keys: subscription_no -> subscription.subscription_no (logical)

approval_history.description: Approval audit trail for subscription approvals with notes and approver identity.
foreign_keys: subscription_no -> subscription.subscription_no (logical)

payment_history.description: Payment transaction log for subscriptions including payment mode, transaction id, and supporting document.
foreign_keys: subscription_no -> subscription.subscription_no (logical)

subscription_master.description: Master configuration for subscription/document categories and renewal filtering by company.

column_descriptions.company_name: Company name for which master settings apply.

make_quotation.description: Quotation document header including consignor/consignee details, banking details, line items (JSON), PDF output, and grand total.
foreign_keys: Potential link to clients/client master via company_name/consignee fields (not enforced)
prepared_by may link to users.user_name (not enforced)

column_descriptions.items: JSONB list of quoted items (code/name/qty/gst/rate etc.).

clients.description: Client/customer master data for sales with salesperson ownership and client type segmentation.
foreign_keys: sales_person_id likely references a salesperson master/users.id (not provided; not enforced)

client_followups.description: Sales follow-up call log per client with actual order outcomes and next calling date.
foreign_keys: client_name may map to clients.client_name (text match; not enforced)
sales_person may map to users.user_name (text match; not enforced)

enquiry_tracker.description: Enquiry follow-up tracker capturing status changes and order receipt outcomes over time (likely an event/history table per enquiry).
foreign_keys: enquiry_no likely references fms_leads.lead_no or enquiry_to_order.en_enquiry_no (not enforced)

column_descriptions.id: Primary key (nullable in schema report; likely generated).
column_descriptions.if_no_relevant_reason_status: Reason status when order not received.
column_descriptions.calling_days: Number of days between calls or SLA for calling.

fms_leads.description: Lead management (CRM) raw lead intake table capturing lead details, planned/actual actions, enquiry conversion, and order receipt data; heavily denormalized with many text fields.
foreign_keys: lead_no used across trackers (enquiry_tracker/leads_tracker); not enforced
sc_name/salesperson_name/lead_receiver_name are names (potential users mapping)

column_descriptions.item_qty: JSON text list of requested items/qty.

leads_tracker.description: Lead tracking/history table (appears corrupted/misaligned in sample), intended to store status/stage changes and next call scheduling for leads.
foreign_keys: lead_no intended to reference fms_leads.lead_no (but sample shows mis-mapped columns)

dropdown.description: Centralized dropdown/master values store for CRM/quotation/enquiry forms (acts as configuration/reference data rather than transactional records).

column_descriptions.quotation_shared_by: Allowed values for who shared quotation.
column_descriptions.not_received_reason_status: Allowed reason categories for not receiving order.

enq_erp.description: ERP-style enquiry demand capture by item type/size/thickness with quantity and sales executive attribution (structured, analytics-friendly).
foreign_keys: size/thickness could map to size_master (not enforced)

size_master.description: Product size master mapping item_type + size + thickness combinations (likely for pipe/sections).

sms_register.description: Steel Melting Shop (SMS) batch/register log capturing heat/batch metadata (sequence, ladle, furnace), temperatures, responsible staff, and photos.
foreign_keys: unique_code and sequence_number relate to downstream tables (qc_lab_samples, hot_coil, pipe_mill, re_coiler) by batch code patterns; not enforced

qc_lab_samples.description: Quality Control lab test results for SMS batches including chemistry values (C, Mn, S, P) and report images.
foreign_keys: sms_batch_code likely references sms_register.unique_code (logical)
sequence_code likely references sms_register.sequence_number (logical)
laddle_number likely references sms_register.laddle_number (logical)

hot_coil.description: Hot coil production/inspection log tied to SMS batch codes, recording temperatures and staff on shift with photo evidence.
foreign_keys: sms_short_code / unique_code likely references sms_register.unique_code (logical)

re_coiler.description: Recoiler operational log (normalized table) capturing recoiling shift details tied to hot coil batch codes.
foreign_keys: hot_coiler_short_code likely references hot_coil.sms_short_code (logical)
unique_code likely references pipe_mill.recoiler_short_code or re_coiler.unique_code in other table variants (logical)

column_descriptions.hot_coiler_short_code: Upstream hot coil batch short code.

re_coile.description: Alternate/older recoiler table variant (note spelling) without shift/updated fields; likely legacy.

pipe_mill.description: Pipe mill production log capturing production run metadata, equipment checks (turbine/blower), staffing, product attributes, and batch linkage codes.
foreign_keys: recoiler_short_code likely references re_coiler.unique_code (logical)
unique_code likely derived from sms_register.unique_code + sequence (logical)

column_descriptions.sample_timestamp: When the production log was captured.

laddle_checklist.description: Ladle checklist/inspection log for SMS/CCM operations with completion flags for cleaning and equipment readiness, including personnel names and plate life.
foreign_keys: unique_code may align with sms_register.unique_code or an independent checklist code (format C-XXXX); not enforced

column_descriptions.id: Row id (nullable in schema; not reliable as PK).

tundish_checklist.description: Tundish preparation/handover checklist log for continuous casting operations with done/not-done checks and staffing.

patching_checklist.description: Furnace/crucible patching checklist and process log including material checks, times, and quality/safety boolean checks with unique code and photos.

laddle_return.description: Ladle return log capturing temperatures, operators, and reasons when ladle returns from furnace/CCM, with photos and unique code.

projects.description: Project master for civil/engineering projects with schedule and client/location.

project_structure.description: Work breakdown structure (WBS) hierarchy for projects (areas/levels) with parent-child relationships.
foreign_keys: project_id -> projects.project_id (intended)
parent_id -> project_structure.structure_id (self-referential hierarchy)

column_descriptions.parent_id: Parent structure node id (null for root).
column_descriptions.name: Node name (e.g., First Floor).

activities.description: Project activities/tasks under a structure node with planned quantities, units, due dates, and priority.
foreign_keys: structure_id -> project_structure.structure_id (intended)

daily_progress.description: Daily progress reporting against project activities including quantity completed, labour count, and remarks.
foreign_keys: activity_id -> activities.activity_id (intended)

materials.description: Material master/inventory snapshot including current stock and minimum threshold for alerts.

material_inward.description: Inventory inward transactions for materials including supplier and date.
foreign_keys: material_id -> materials.material_id (intended)

material_consumption.description: Material consumption transactions linked to project activities for costing and progress correlation.
foreign_keys: material_id -> materials.material_id (intended)
activity_id -> activities.activity_id (intended)

locations.description: Master list of physical locations/areas used for housekeeping/tasks and possibly asset placement.

departments.description: Department master list with optional HOD and contact details.

form_responses.description: Asset/equipment registry capturing machine metadata (serial, tag, purchase, warranty, maintenance schedule) and document links (manual, bill).
foreign_keys: department may align with departments.department (text match; not enforced)
serial_no/tag_no referenced by maintenance_task_assign and repair_system (logical)

column_descriptions.serial_no: Asset serial number (also used as tag in sample).
column_descriptions.user_allot: User allotted / assigned user (field values suggest misused).

working_day_calender.description: Calendar table defining working dates with day-of-week labels and week/month numbers (likely for reporting and SLA calculations).

request.description: General request workflow table used for travel requests and recruitment/manpower requests (multi-purpose form).
foreign_keys: employee_code may map to users.employee_id (not enforced)

ticket_book.description: Ticket/booking expense capture including bill details, amounts, and request vs booked employee codes.
foreign_keys: request_employee_code / booked_employee_code may map to users.employee_id (not enforced)

payment_fms.description: Generic payment workflow tracker (FMS) with staged dates and delays, attachments, and payment type; likely used for vendor/internal payments outside subscriptions.

column_descriptions.unique_no: Business unique number for the payment request.

master.description: Legacy/simple task master capturing doer, departments, priority, and task type/status (likely an earlier maintenance/task module).

indent_requests.description: Indent request header table (separate from indent line items) capturing indent number, department, requester, priority, and status.
foreign_keys: indent_no may relate to indent.indent_number (not enforced)

indent_items.description: Indent line items for an indent header request (if using indent_requests module), including available stock snapshot.
foreign_keys: indent_id -> indent_requests.id (likely) OR indent.id (possible); ambiguous

repair_followup.description: Repair gate-pass follow-up tracker with multi-stage planned/actual dates and delays for repair lifecycle (likely store/maintenance coordination).

plant_visitor.description: Internal request for bringing visitors to plant (planned date range, counts) with approval workflow fields.
foreign_keys: employee_code/approv_employee_code may map to users.employee_id (not enforced)

all_loans.description: Loan master tracker for company loans including EMI schedule, dates, documents, and remarks.

request_forclosure.description: Loan foreclosure request log including requester and request date.
foreign_keys: serial_no may relate to all_loans or collect_noc serial numbers (not enforced)

column_descriptions.serial_no: Business serial number for foreclosure request.

collect_noc.description: NOC collection tracker for loan closure including closure request date and whether NOC collected.

resume_request.description: Recruitment candidate pipeline capturing candidate details, resume link, interview planned/actual times, and statuses.
foreign_keys: req_id may map to request.request_no or internal requisition id (not enforced)

login.description: Legacy authentication table (separate from users) storing credentials and basic profile fields; likely deprecated.
foreign_keys: employee_id may map to users.employee_id (not enforced)

suggested_semantic_schema:
DOMAIN OVERVIEW
- This is an enterprise operations database for a manufacturing/steel plant with modules: Task Management (checklists/delegations/assignments), Maintenance & Repairs, Sales CRM (leads/enquiries/quotations), Store & Purchase (indents/GRNs/repair follow-up), Production & Quality logs (SMS/CCM/pipe mill/hot coil/recoiler), HR/Admin (users, leave, visitors/gate pass, travel/tickets), and Subscription/Document management.

ENTITY GROUPS & HOW TO QUERY

1) USERS & ACCESS
Tables:
- users (PK: id)
Key fields: user_name, employee_id, department, division, role, status.
Notes: Many operational tables store person names (given_by, doer_name, sales_person_name) as TEXT instead of user_id. Prefer joining by users.employee_id when available; otherwise join by users.user_name with caution.

2) TASK MANAGEMENT (CHECKLIST / DELEGATION / GENERAL TASKS)
Tables:
- checklist (PK: task_id): recurring checklist tasks; completion in status/user_status_checklist and admin confirmation in admin_done; submission_date and delay for SLA.
- delegation (PK: task_id): delegated tasks with planned_date/submission_date/status.
- delegation_done (PK: id, FK logical: task_id -> delegation.task_id): log of status changes, extensions, reasons.
- assign_task (PK: id): general tasks (often housekeeping) with department=physical location, doer_name2, frequency, submission_date.
Common analytics:
- On-time completion = submission_date <= task_start_date/planned_date (use delay where provided).
- Completion rate by division/department/given_by/name.

3) MAINTENANCE & REPAIR
Tables:
- maintenance_task_assign (PK: id): preventive maintenance tasks for machines; fields include task_no, serial_no, task_start_date, actual_date, task_status, maintenance_cost.
- repair_system (PK: id): repair workflow with vendor, transport, staged planned/actual dates, bill fields.
- form_responses (PK: id): asset registry; join via serial_no/tag_no to maintenance_task_assign.serial_no and repair_system.serial_no (string match).
Common analytics:
- Mean delay by department/division and priority.
- Maintenance completion % and cost by machine/department.

4) STORE & PURCHASE
Tables:
- indent (PK: id): indent/requisition line items; planned_1/actual_1/time_delay_1; request_status and gm_approval.
- indent_approvals (PK: id, intended FK: indent_id -> indent.id): approval actions.
- store_grn (PK inferred: grn_no): GRN and bill approval workflow flags.
- repair_followup (PK: id): gate pass repair follow-up stages.
Common analytics:
- Approval cycle time = actual_1 - planned_1 (or time_delay_1).
- Rejection rate by department/group_name.

5) SALES / CRM
Tables:
- fms_leads (PK: id text): lead intake and conversion to enquiry/order; many fields stored as TEXT including dates.
- enquiry_to_order (PK: id): structured enquiry lifecycle with follow-ups and order terms; item_qty stored as JSON-like TEXT.
- enquiry_tracker (PK: id): status history per enquiry_no.
- make_quotation (PK: id): quotation header; items in JSONB; grand_total and pdf_url.
- clients (PK: client_id) and client_followups (PK: followup_id): client master + follow-up logs.
- enq_erp (PK: id) and size_master (PK: id): structured demand by size/thickness.
Common analytics:
- Pipeline by enquiry_status/current_stage.
- Follow-up compliance: next_call_date/time vs current date.
- Quote totals from make_quotation.grand_total; item-level analysis requires JSONB parsing.

6) PRODUCTION & QUALITY LOGS (SMS/CCM/PIPE MILL)
Tables:
- sms_register (PK: id): heat/batch log; unique_code is the SMS batch short code.
- qc_lab_samples (PK: id): chemistry results; join logically on sms_batch_code = sms_register.unique_code and sequence/laddle/furnace when needed.
- hot_coil (PK: id): hot coil log; sms_short_code/unique_code relate to SMS batch.
- re_coiler (PK: id) and re_coile (legacy): recoiler log; unique_code like 24B01B.
- pipe_mill (PK: id): pipe mill runs; unique_code and recoiler_short_code relate to upstream.
- laddle_checklist (PK suggested: unique_code) and tundish_checklist (PK: id): equipment readiness checklists.
- patching_checklist (PK: id): furnace patching.
Common analytics:
- Batch traceability: sms_register.unique_code -> qc_lab_samples.sms_batch_code -> hot_coil.sms_short_code -> re_coiler.unique_code -> pipe_mill.recoiler_short_code/unique_code.
- QC averages by batch/furnace/sequence.

7) HR / ADMIN
Tables:
- leave_request (PK: id, intended FK user_id -> users.id): leave workflow.
- visitors (PK: id) and person_to_meet (PK: id): visitor gate pass.
- gatepass (PK: id): employee outpass.
- request (PK: id): travel/recruitment requests.
- ticket_book (PK: id): ticket booking expenses.

QUERYING GUIDELINES FOR TEXT-TO-SQL
- Prefer timestamp/date columns for filtering: created_at, sample_timestamp, enquiry_date, quotation_date, task_start_date, submission_date.
- Beware inconsistent data types: many CRM fields are TEXT dates in fms_leads/enquiry_tracker/leads_tracker. Cast when needed (e.g., to_date(next_call_date,'YYYY-MM-DD')).
- Names are not stable keys. When joining people, use users.employee_id when possible; otherwise join on users.user_name carefully (case/spacing variants).
- JSON fields: make_quotation.items is JSONB; enquiry_to_order.item_qty is TEXT containing JSON; parsing may require JSON functions and cleanup.
- Status fields are inconsistent in casing (Yes/yes/Done/done). Normalize with LOWER(status).

CANONICAL DIMENSIONS (recommended for analytics)
- dim_user (from users)
- dim_department (from departments)
- dim_location (from locations)
- dim_division (from users/division fields)
- dim_date (from working_day_calender)

CANONICAL FACTS
- fact_checklist_completion (from checklist)
- fact_delegation_tasks and fact_delegation_events (delegation + delegation_done)
- fact_assign_tasks (assign_task)
- fact_maintenance_tasks (maintenance_task_assign)
- fact_repairs (repair_system)
- fact_indents (indent) and fact_indent_approvals (indent_approvals)
- fact_grn (store_grn)
- fact_sales_leads (fms_leads) and fact_enquiries (enquiry_to_order/enquiry_tracker)
- fact_quotation (make_quotation)
- fact_production_batches (sms_register/hot_coil/re_coiler/pipe_mill) and fact_qc_tests (qc_lab_samples)
